acceptance_targets: [ch01-craft-001] PASS if the revised section anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:293 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:310 contains an on-page dialogue beat where institutional staff state a specific liability term, Marisol responds in-scene, and the block ends with an immediate physical or logistical consequence; FAIL if this section remains primarily email/summary text.

[ch01-booker-001] PASS if the final paragraph anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:319 ends on an immediate in-scene consequence and does not close on a future appointment/objective statement; FAIL if the closing beat remains a forward-looking setup line.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised section anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:293 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:310 contains an on-page dialogue beat where institutional staff state a specific liability term, Marisol responds in-scene, and the block ends with an immediate physical or logistical consequence; FAIL if this section remains primarily email/summary text.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the legal-risk peak: after explicit warning of personal exposure, the narrative retreats into email text and fact-log summary instead of rendering the risk-ack confrontation on-page. This defuses tension at the exact moment consequence should become embodied.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the post-warning block at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:293, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:297, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:304, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:310 as a live Conference B exchange. Keep one short quoted email if needed, but stage the confrontation with Legal/Security in-scene and force Marisol to answer one non-negotiable condition in person.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the final paragraph anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:319 ends on an immediate in-scene consequence and does not close on a future appointment/objective statement; FAIL if the closing beat remains a forward-looking setup line.
findings.problem: The chapter closes by previewing the next interview objective instead of landing the immediate consequence of the decision, softening chapter-end impact.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the ending around the immediate post-email consequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:314 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:319. Keep forward motion, but end on a present-scene cost (institutional control, bodily fallout, or relational rupture) rather than a planning line.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When choosing between reputationally safe abstraction and specific discomfort, choose specificity. Render addiction, sexuality, deceit, class shame, and grief at lived scale. In edge cases, preserve character truth and causal accountability over reader comfort or institutional decorum.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene demands bodily, moral, or economic specificity, euphemistic phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with concrete stakes and observable consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; full forms appear only for emphasis, ritual language, or character-specific formal posture

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density and pressure modes: pair high-conflict chapters with quiet domestic or documentary chapters, vary dialogue-to-interiority ratios, and prevent identical chapter skeletons in consecutive sequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: contemporary literary diction with class-aware, borough-specific concreteness; avoid prestige-neutral workshop language
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Marisol, with rare brief distance expansions for institutional scenes
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternating tensile compression and reflective extension; line-level cadence should carry emotional pressure, not just information
